finding: Pure task-ledger or `.atm/history` record updates still often require borrowing a task-bound claim/release/commit dance even when the operator is only syncing import metadata, transition records, or benign governance history.
expectedBehavior: ATM should provide an official record-commit lane for `tasks import --write` and pure `.atm/history` maintenance that preserves evidence, actor identity, and protected-state checks without requiring a fake delivery session.
evidenceOrRepro: `TASK-AAO-0153` adds `node atm.mjs git record-commit`: it requires explicit actor/message, rejects `--task`, `--auto-stage`, `--defer-foreign-staged`, and `--no-verify`, accepts only low-risk `.atm/history` record files, appends `ATM-Record-Commit: true`, and still stages same-commit git-head provenance through the governed commit wrapper.
followUp: Keep `tests/cli/git-record-commit.test.ts` wired into `validate:cli`; it proves task ledger records can commit without a task session while source files and closure packets fail closed.
